title: The best tools for migrating from Evernote to Notion

interlinking_suggestions.title: The Best Note-Taking and Knowledge Management Apps for Freelancers
A Guide to Notion for Freelance Business Management

author.name: Marcus Thorne
author.bio: A former tech COO turned 'Business-of-One' consultant, Marcus is obsessed with efficiency. He writes about optimizing workflows, leveraging technology, and building resilient systems for solo entrepreneurs.

key_takeaways: Before migrating, create a full, independent backup of your critical data in both ENEX and HTML formats to de-risk the process and ensure zero data loss.
Execute the transfer in controlled phases, starting with a single low-risk notebook as a pilot test before moving mission-critical operational data.
Avoid recreating your old folder structure; instead, consolidate imported notes into a few master relational databases (e.g., Projects, Clients, Knowledge Base) to build an interconnected system.
Build a 'CEO Dashboard' using linked database views to create a single, dynamic command center that surfaces your most active projects, client follow-ups, and tasks.
summary: Migrating from Evernote to Notion is a daunting operational upgrade for professionals whose needs have outgrown a simple digital filing cabinet. This playbook provides a structured, three-phase methodology that treats the process like a professional data migration, advising you to first de-risk the move with audits and backups before executing a precise, phased transfer. The key outcome is the transformation of a static archive into a dynamic business command center, where relational databases and a central dashboard provide interconnected, actionable intelligence.
ai_overview_answer:
To professionally migrate from Evernote to Notion, you should follow a structured, three-phase process focused on preparation, transfer, and reorganization. First, de-risk the migration by auditing your notebooks into tiers based on business impact and creating a full, independent backup using both ENEX and HTML file formats. Before transferring, standardize your notebook naming conventions to align with Notion’s database-centric structure. Execute the transfer in phases using Notion’s official importer, starting with a low-risk notebook as a test, and keep the manual ENEX file import method as a reliable fallback.

After each import, conduct a post-migration audit to verify that all notes and attachments have transferred correctly. Once your data is in Notion, avoid recreating your old folder structure; instead, consolidate notes into a few master relational databases like 'Projects,' 'Clients,' and 'Knowledge Base.' Connect these databases to create an integrated system that was impossible in Evernote. Finally, build a central dashboard with linked database views to create an actionable command center for managing your work.

itemListElement.name: The best tools for migrating from Evernote to Notion

@graph.headline: The best tools for migrating from Evernote to Notion
@graph.name: How to Migrate from Evernote to Notion: A Professional's Playbook
@graph.description: This playbook provides a structured, three-phase methodology for migrating from Evernote to Notion, treating the process like a professional data migration to ensure zero data loss and transform your static archive into a dynamic business command center.

step.name: Phase 1: De-Risk the Migration and Ensure Zero Data Loss
step.text: Before you move a single note, you must secure your assets and build a deliberate plan. This initial phase is entirely focused on preparation and risk mitigation, ensuring the core of your business is protected before the technical transfer begins.

itemListElement.text: Conduct a Business Impact Audit: Categorize your Evernote notebooks into tiers (Mission-Critical, High-Value, General Archive) based on their immediate importance to your operations to create a phased roadmap.

Create a Business Continuity Backup: Perform a complete export of your Tier 1 and Tier 2 assets using both ENEX and HTML formats from the Evernote desktop app. Store these files in a secure, separate cloud storage location.

Standardize Your Structure for Notion: Review your existing notebook titles and establish a consistent naming convention (e.g., `Client: [Client Name]`) to align with Notion’s database-centric architecture.
Archive, Don't Just Delete: Move all outdated-but-necessary notes into a single 'Master Archive' notebook in Evernote to shrink the scope of your main migration effort.

step.name: Phase 2: Execute the Transfer with Professional Precision
step.text: With your data audited, backed up, and structured, the technical transfer becomes a straightforward, procedural task. We will approach this with a primary method selected for efficiency and a reliable fallback plan to maintain absolute control.

itemListElement.text: Use the Official Notion Importer: In Notion, navigate to `Settings & Members` > `Import` and authorize your Evernote account. Execute a phased migration, starting with a single, low-risk notebook as a test.
Use Manual Export/Import as a Fallback: If the official importer fails, export a notebook from the Evernote desktop app as an ENEX file and upload it directly into Notion's import tool.
Perform a Post-Migration Audit: Immediately after each notebook import, verify completeness, check attachments, and review formatting to ensure all data has been successfully moved.

step.name: Phase 3: Build Your New Business Command Center
step.text: With your data verified, the final phase is about architecting a more powerful operational system. This is where you evolve beyond a chaotic collection of notes and build an integrated Command Center, transforming static information into dynamic intelligence.

itemListElement.text: Transform Folders into Relational Databases: Consolidate imported notes into a few master databases (e.g., `Projects`, `Clients`, `Knowledge Base`) and use the 'Relation' property to connect them.
Implement a System to Manage Actionability: Use a framework like P.A.R.A. (Projects, Areas, Resources, Archives) within your master databases to organize information based on its actionability.
Create Your CEO Dashboard: Build a central page using Linked Databases to create filtered views of your most critical information, such as active projects, client follow-ups, and weekly tasks.

mainEntity.name: Is it safe to migrate from Evernote to Notion?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, provided you follow a professional protocol. The process is secure if you: 1) Create a full, independent backup of your data as a business continuity plan. 2) Use a phased approach, starting with low-risk data. 3) Perform a post-migration audit to verify the transfer of critical assets. This structured approach removes uncertainty and ensures control.

mainEntity.name: What gets lost when migrating from Evernote to Notion?

acceptedAnswer.text:
While core content like text, images, and attachments migrate well, certain Evernote-specific features will not transfer. Expect to manually adjust:
- **Note Creation Dates:** Often reset to the date of import.
- **Complex Formatting:** Intricate tables or highlights may not render correctly.
- **Internal Evernote Note Links:** These will break and must be re-linked manually.
- **Proprietary Features:** Evernote functions like reminders, location tags, and presentation mode do not have direct equivalents and will be lost.

mainEntity.name: How long does the Evernote to Notion migration take?

acceptedAnswer.text: The duration depends entirely on the size of your library. An account with a few hundred notes may take only minutes. A power user with thousands of notes and large attachments should budget several hours. The process can stall, which is why having a manual backup and a phased migration plan is non-negotiable.

mainEntity.name: Can I migrate just one notebook from Evernote to Notion?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, and this is the recommended professional strategy. The Notion importer allows you to select specific notebooks. Starting with a single, non-critical notebook serves as a pilot test, allowing you to understand the process and see how your data appears in Notion before you commit to moving your mission-critical systems.

mainEntity.name: How do I organize my Evernote notebooks in Notion after importing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Avoid recreating your old folder structure. The best practice is to consolidate your imported notes into a few master databases (e.g., `Clients`, `Projects`, `Knowledge Base`). Then, use Notion's `Relation` property to connect items—linking projects to clients, for example. This creates an interconnected system that provides far more insight than Evernote's siloed notebooks ever could.

mainEntity.name: Should I clean up Evernote before migrating?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Absolutely. A pre-migration cleanup is a high-leverage activity that dramatically improves the outcome. Focus on three key actions:
1. **Delete:** Purge old, irrelevant, and duplicate notes to reduce clutter and import time.
2. **Standardize:** Unify your tagging system with a consistent naming convention. These tags can become useful properties in Notion.
3. **Merge:** Combine smaller, related notes into more comprehensive documents to make your new knowledge base more robust from day one.

body.content: The decision to migrate from Evernote to Notion is rarely about a simple feature comparison. It’s an acknowledgment that your operational needs have evolved. What once served as a reliable digital filing cabinet now feels restrictive, and the prospect of moving years of accumulated knowledge feels both necessary and daunting. This is not an administrative task; it is a strategic maneuver to upgrade your business's core operating system.

This playbook converts that operational anxiety into structured control. We will treat this process with the seriousness of a corporate data migration, moving beyond a simple "lift and shift" to architect a more intelligent, integrated, and resilient command center for your work. We will proceed in three phases: securing your assets, executing a precise transfer, and building your new headquarters.
Phase 1: De-Risk the Migration and Ensure Zero Data Loss

Before you move a single note, you must secure your assets and build a deliberate plan. This initial phase is entirely focused on preparation and risk mitigation, ensuring the core of your business is protected before the technical transfer begins.
Conduct a Business Impact Audit

A professional never flies blind. Your first step is to categorize your Evernote notebooks into tiers based on their immediate importance to your operations. This audit forces you to handle your most valuable information with the highest level of care and creates a clear, phased roadmap for the migration itself.

rows: Active client contracts, financial records, strategic plans, SOPs
Intellectual property, project research, client development notes
Old project notes, reading lists, personal reminders, saved articles

body.content: You will migrate and validate all Tier 1 assets before moving on, guaranteeing your core operation is secure in its new home before anything else is touched.
Create a Business Continuity Backup

Relying solely on an application's import tool for mission-critical data is not a strategy; it's a gamble. Your insurance policy is a full, independent backup. Before initiating any transfer, perform a complete export of your Tier 1 and Tier 2 assets using both of the following formats from the Evernote desktop app:

items: **ENEX (Evernote Export) files:** This XML-based format is crucial for data integrity. It preserves your core note content, attachments, and metadata, making it ideal for importing into Notion or other applications.
**HTML files:** While not importable, HTML exports are universally accessible in any web browser. They serve as a vital, human-readable reference for visual verification.
**Store Securely & Separately:** Place these exported files in a secure, separate cloud storage location (e.g., Google Drive, Dropbox), not on the same machine you're migrating from.

body.content: This procedure ensures that no matter what happens during the technical transfer, your data is safe and recoverable.
Standardize Your Structure for Notion

A migration is a rare opportunity to fix past mistakes. Evernote’s unstructured nature often leads to chaos. Before you move, standardize your notebook and tag conventions to align with Notion’s database-centric architecture.
Review your existing notebook titles and establish a consistent naming convention. For example:

items: `Client: [Client Name]`
`Project: [Project Code]`
`Knowledge: [Topic]`
`Admin: [Category]`

body.content: This disciplined approach transforms the migration into a strategic restructuring. A notebook named `Project: Phoenix` will arrive in Notion as a clean database, ready to be integrated into a larger, relational system—a task made ten times harder with generic names like "Misc Notes."
Archive, Don't Just Delete

Finally, address the clutter. Many professionals have years of notes that are no longer active but cannot be deleted due to compliance or record-keeping requirements. Do not complicate your primary migration with this data. Create a single notebook in Evernote called "Master Archive." Move all outdated-but-necessary notes into it. You can then either migrate this large archive last or leave it in Evernote as a low-cost storage solution. This shrinks the scope of your main effort, allowing you to focus on high-value data.
Phase 2: Execute the Transfer with Professional Precision

With your data audited, backed up, and structured, the technical transfer becomes a straightforward, procedural task. We will approach this with a primary method selected for efficiency and a reliable fallback plan to maintain absolute control.
Method 1: The Official Notion Importer (Primary Path)
For most users, Notion's built-in migration tool is the most direct route. It connects directly to your Evernote account to handle the transfer.

items: **Initiate the Import:** In Notion, navigate to `Settings & Members` in your sidebar, then select `Import`.
**Authorize Your Account:** Choose `Evernote` from the list. You will be prompted to sign in to your Evernote account and authorize Notion to access your data.

**Execute a Phased Migration:** This is the most critical step. **Do not** import all your notebooks at once. Begin by selecting a single, low-risk Tier 3 notebook. This test run allows you to verify the process and see how your data appears in Notion without risking mission-critical information.

body.content: Once initiated, Notion will pull the data, and your imported notebook will appear as a new private page in your sidebar. Depending on the size, this can take anywhere from a few minutes to several hours.
Method 2: The Manual Export/Import (The Essential Fallback)
Sometimes, the official importer can struggle with very large notebooks or numerous attachments. This is not a crisis; it is a contingency. A professional is defined by their Plan B.

items: **Export from Evernote:** In the Evernote desktop app, right-click the notebook you want to move, select `Export...`, and choose the **ENEX file format** (`.enex`). Save this file to a secure, easily accessible location.
**Import into Notion:** In Notion's `Import` tool, select `Evernote` and you will be given the option to upload the `.enex` file directly.

body.content: This manual method gives you granular control over one notebook at a time and serves as a reliable workaround if the automated connection fails.
Perform a Post-Migration Audit
Your data is not successfully moved until you have verified it. Immediately after each notebook import—especially for your Tier 1 assets—conduct a swift but thorough audit. Trust, but verify.

items: **Check for Completeness:** Does the number of notes in the Notion database match the original Evernote notebook?
**Verify Attachments:** Open several notes that contain images, PDFs, or other files. Confirm they are present and accessible.
**Review Formatting:** While most basic formatting transfers well, complex layouts may not. Check your most important documents for readability.

**Acknowledge Known Limitations:** Be aware that internal links between Evernote notes will break. They must be manually re-linked once all relevant data is inside Notion. This is an expected outcome, not a failure of the process.

body.content: Phase 3: Build Your New Business Command Center

With your data verified, the final phase is about architecting a more powerful operational system. This is where you evolve beyond a chaotic collection of notes and build an integrated Command Center, transforming static information into dynamic intelligence.
Transform Folders into Relational Databases
Your imported Evernote notebooks now exist in Notion as separate pages or simple databases. Leaving them this way misses the point of the upgrade. The true power lies in consolidation and connection.
Create a few master databases that reflect the core pillars of your business. For most professionals, this means starting with three:

items: **`Projects`**: For any initiative with a defined goal and deadline.
**`Clients`**: A directory of every client, containing all related contact and contract information.
**`Knowledge Base`**: A library for resources, research, and evergreen intellectual property.

body.content: Move your imported notes into these master databases. Then, connect them. In your `Projects` database, add a "Relation" property that links to your `Clients` database. Now, you can associate every project with a specific client, creating a web of interconnected intelligence that was impossible in Evernote's folder structure.
Implement a System to Manage Actionability

With your information consolidated, you need a system to organize it based on relevance. A powerful but chaotic tool hinders productivity. One of the most effective frameworks for this is Tiago Forte's P.A.R.A. method, which organizes information based on its actionability.
P.A.R.A. stands for:

items: **Projects:** Short-term efforts you're actively working on.
**Areas:** Long-term responsibilities with a standard to maintain (e.g., "Business Finances").
**Resources:** Topics of ongoing interest that aren't immediately actionable.
**Archives:** Inactive items from the other three categories.

body.content: Implement this directly within your new master databases. Your `Projects` database *is* your "P." Your `Knowledge Base` serves as your "R" for Resources. Use a "Status" property to distinguish between active "Projects" and ongoing "Areas," and to move completed items to an "Archive" status, removing them from your active views without deleting them. This ensures the information you see daily is precisely what you need to act on.
Create Your CEO Dashboard

This is the culmination of your efforts: a single, dynamic page that gives you complete command over your operation. A CEO Dashboard is a living hub built from **Linked Databases**—filtered views of your master databases.
Start with a new page titled "Command Center." Then, begin pulling in views of your most critical information by typing `/linked view of database`.

rows: Provides an at-a-glance view of your current workload and priorities.
`Next Contact Date` is "On or Before Today"
Ensures no client relationship or opportunity falls through the cracks.
Creates a focused task list without the noise of future or delegated tasks.
A quick-capture view for logging new ideas before they get lost.

body.content: By building this dashboard, you finally achieve the promise of this migration: transforming scattered notes into a centralized, actionable system for running your business.
Frequently Asked Questions

items:
**Is it safe to migrate from Evernote to Notion?**
Yes, provided you follow a professional protocol. The process is secure if you: 1) Create a full, independent backup of your data as a business continuity plan. 2) Use a phased approach, starting with low-risk data. 3) Perform a post-migration audit to verify the transfer of critical assets. This structured approach removes uncertainty and ensures control.

**What gets lost when migrating from Evernote to Notion?**
While core content like text, images, and attachments migrate well, certain Evernote-specific features will not transfer. Expect to manually adjust:
*   **Note Creation Dates:** Often reset to the date of import.
*   **Complex Formatting:** Intricate tables or highlights may not render correctly.
*   **Internal Evernote Note Links:** These will break and must be re-linked manually.
*   **Proprietary Features:** Evernote functions like reminders, location tags, and presentation mode do not have direct equivalents and will be lost.

**How long does the Evernote to Notion migration take?**
The duration depends entirely on the size of your library. An account with a few hundred notes may take only minutes. A power user with thousands of notes and large attachments should budget several hours. The process can stall, which is why having a manual backup and a phased migration plan is non-negotiable.

**Can I migrate just one notebook from Evernote to Notion?**
Yes, and this is the recommended professional strategy. The Notion importer allows you to select specific notebooks. Starting with a single, non-critical notebook serves as a pilot test, allowing you to understand the process and see how your data appears in Notion before you commit to moving your mission-critical systems.

**How do I organize my Evernote notebooks in Notion after importing?**
Avoid recreating your old folder structure. The best practice is to consolidate your imported notes into a few master databases (e.g., `Clients`, `Projects`, `Knowledge Base`). Then, use Notion's `Relation` property to connect items—linking projects to clients, for example. This creates an interconnected system that provides far more insight than Evernote's siloed notebooks ever could.

**Should I clean up Evernote before migrating?**
Absolutely. A pre-migration cleanup is a high-leverage activity that dramatically improves the outcome. Focus on three key actions:
1.  **Delete:** Purge old, irrelevant, and duplicate notes to reduce clutter and import time.
2.  **Standardize:** Unify your tagging system with a consistent naming convention. These tags can become useful properties in Notion.
3.  **Merge:** Combine smaller, related notes into more comprehensive documents to make your new knowledge base more robust from day one.

body.content: Conclusion: You Haven't Just Switched Tools—You've Upgraded Your Operation

By following this playbook, you have executed a strategic upgrade of your business's core operating system. This was a deliberate choice to evolve from a static archive into a dynamic, integrated workspace. Where Evernote served as a digital filing cabinet, Notion now serves as your command center.

The professional diligence you applied—auditing assets, creating backups, and verifying the transfer—was an act of taking control over the central nervous system of your business. The real victory isn't seeing your old notes in a new interface. It's the moment you link a project to a client, creating a relational web of information that was impossible before. This prevents critical intelligence from being lost in isolated folders and empowers you to make better, faster decisions.

You are now equipped not just with a better tool, but with a superior operational methodology. The CEO Dashboard you built provides a single source of truth, defending against the fragmentation that plagues so many professionals. You have turned a collection of disparate information into an engine for productivity and growth. You haven't just changed where you work; you've fundamentally changed *how* you work.
